issue.title: Retire the temporary WP_Http::buildCookieHeader public shell

artifacts.role: WPHX-312.63 runner that installs the compiler-emitted grouped WP_Http shell
ownership manifest for the Haxe-owned buildCookieHeader assembly boundary
grouped WP_Http compiler manifest proving unsupported=[]
typed WPHX PHP source for the grouped parser/header/cookie shell
compiler operations note documenting the retired temporary shell
program progress checkpoint for this compiler evidence

claims: The WPHX-312.63 WP_Http::buildCookieHeader candidate now uses the WPHX compiler-emitted grouped original-path class-wp-http.php public shell.

The runner verifies the WPHX emission manifest records class:WP_Http with unsupported=[] and verifies public static function buildCookieHeader(&$r) exists before running the oracle/candidate behavior probe.
The previous copied or runner-patched temporary public PHP shell is no longer the authority for the buildCookieHeader boundary.
non_claims: This does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership.
This does not claim WP_Http::request, live HTTP transport, installed WordPress distribution behavior, or broad generated public PHP replacement.
This does not claim WPHX PHP is already a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
